surface_verification.justification: Infra/doc change: CI workflow YAML (third-party action SHA-pinning, Gitleaks tarball SHA256 verification) + SECURITY.md wording. No application/Python source, no UI, no API/store/SSE surface (backend-affects-frontend rule N/A). Workflow invariants are covered by test_workflow_shape (14), CI integration gating tests (5), the check_ci_gate_coverage guard, the CI template-registry test, and yaml.safe_load on all 4 workflows (all green); the runtime hardening is exercised by this PR's own CI + Security Scan workflow runs.

behaviors.behavior: Workflows stay valid and preserve their security invariants (triggers, permissions, fork-guards, critical-gate, action invocations) after SHA-pinning + Gitleaks edits
behaviors.evidence: yaml.safe_load OK on all 4 workflows; test_workflow_shape.py 14/14 PASSED; test_ci_self_gating.py + test_ci_lint_gate.py 5/5 PASSED; check_ci_gate_coverage.py PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Gitleaks tarball SHA256 is verified before extraction; CI fails closed on a checksum mismatch
behaviors.evidence: SHA256 5bc41815...316e3ba verified against the official gitleaks v8.21.2 checksums.txt; gating runs under the default GitHub-Actions '-eo pipefail' shell via 'sha256sum -c' and is exercised end-to-end by this PR's CI + Security Scan runs
